summary: I was born in a refugee camp in Thailand, and before coming to America, my family and I spent some time in different places within the country. When we arrived in Mae Sot, I met many people who were also planning to go to the United States. The camp there smelled very bad, especially the bathrooms, which I really disliked. Later, we stayed in Bangkok, the capital of Thailand, where I saw many cars and roads for the first time, which made me feel grateful and excited.
cues: first time seeing many cars and roads

summary: My family was resettled in the United States on August 10, 2010, after leaving Thailand. Coming to America was a big change for me, and I remember feeling excited when we arrived in Los Angeles. The roads and cars looked similar to Bangkok, but the language and alphabet on signs were different. After staying briefly in Los Angeles, we moved to Twin Falls, Idaho, and later to St. Paul, Minnesota, where I currently live and attend school.

After arriving in the United States, my family and I first lived in Twin Falls, Idaho. It was a small place with not many Karen people or job opportunities. I had the chance to attend school there, but we only stayed for about six months before moving to St. Paul, Minnesota. Twin Falls was an important first stop in our new life in America.

After living in Twin Falls, my family and I moved to St. Paul, Minnesota, and that's where I am now. It feels like home to me, and I continue my education here. The move was part of our journey to find a better place with more community and opportunities. St. Paul represents a new chapter in my life in the United States.

When we arrived in Mae Sot, Thailand, I met many new people who were also preparing to go to America. The camp there had a very bad smell, especially the bathrooms, which I really disliked and tried to avoid. We only stayed in Mae Sot for about a week before moving on. It was a difficult but important stop on our journey.
cues: bad smell in camp and bathrooms

summary: In Bangkok, the capital of Thailand, I saw many cars and roads for the first time while looking out the window of a double-decker bus. It was exciting and made me feel grateful. My family and I stayed in a hotel there for one night with many other families from the camp. We woke up early the next morning to continue our journey to America.
cues: first time seeing many cars and roads

summary: During our journey to the United States, we flew to Hong Kong after leaving Bangkok. I saw many airplanes there, but I didn’t enjoy the plane ride at all. It was a challenging part of the trip for me. Hong Kong was a brief stop before we continued on to Los Angeles.

When we arrived in Los Angeles, California, I noticed that the city looked similar to Bangkok with many cars and roads, but the signs had different words and alphabets. It made me feel excited again about being in America. We stayed in a hotel there for one night before moving to Twin Falls, Idaho. Los Angeles was our first experience in the United States.
